Title: TRM4 is essential for cellulose deposition in Arabidopsis seed mucilage by maintaining cortical microtubule organization and interacting with CESA3

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TONNEAU1 recruiting motif (TRM) family protein; microtubule-associated protein with TRM motifs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks in seed coat epidermal cells of developing siliques/seeds at 7 DPA (linear cotyledon stage); also detected in leaves, roots, guard cells, hydathodes, receptacles, stigma, petals and filaments
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially accumulated in seed coat epidermal cells during mucilage biosynthesis; highest at 7 DPA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed mucilage of seed coat epidermal cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides structural support for mucilage architecture and extrusion/anchoring of adherent mucilage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes proper cellulose deposition, alignment and organization in seed mucilage; loss causes compact mucilage capsules, short cellulosic rays and disorganized cellulose microfibrils
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stage (especially 7 DPA); oryzalin treatment for microtubule disruption; cellulase treatments (E-CELBA, E-CELTR) used to probe cellulose properties
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TRM4 mutation did not significantly alter total mucilage monosaccharide composition or bulk crystalline cellulose content, indicating a role in cellulose organization rather than overall cellulose quantity; trm3 mutants did not phenocopy trm4 in mucilage
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTRM4 (native, developmental); 35S promoter (constitutive) in complementation/overexpression context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants (trm4 alleles), transgene complementation, qRT-PCR, GUS expression analysis, S4B cellulose staining, CBM3a immunolabeling/ELISA, birefringence analysis, fluorescent protein colocalization with RFP-TUB6, yeast split-ubiquitin assay, BiFC
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix-based microarray seed and silique developmental series from AtGenExpress; coexpression analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious changes in seed size or mature seed coat epidermal morphology reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRM4 is highly expressed in seed coat epidermal cells at the peak stage of mucilage biosynthesis. 2. Loss of TRM4 causes compact mucilage capsules, shorter cellulosic rays, reduced cellulose distribution/crystallinity signals, and disorganized cellulose microfibril alignment without major changes in mucilage monosaccharide composition or bulk crystalline cellulose amount. 3. TRM4 co-localizes with cortical microtubules and directly interacts with CESA3, supporting a role in organizing cellulose deposition through microtubule maintenance.
